misconceptions.display_name: Uses nominal GDP as real GDP
misconceptions.description: Treats nominal GDP as if it were real GDP, forgetting to deflate, so price changes are mistaken for changes in real output (real GDP is inflation-adjusted; nominal is not).
trigger_phrases: nominal gdp is the same as real gdp
no need to adjust for inflation
real and nominal are basically equal

misconceptions.description: Multiplies nominal GDP by the price index (or by the deflator) instead of dividing, inverting the deflation and inflating real GDP instead of adjusting it down — a polarity error against the GDP deflator definition.
trigger_phrases: multiply nominal gdp by the price index
real gdp = nominal gdp times the deflator
scale up by the price index
